question: According to the lecture text, which of the following perfectly defines a "Medication Error"?
options: Any unpreventable physiological complication directly triggered by a correctly processed medication
Any Patient Safety Incident (PSI) where there has been an error in prescribing, preparing, dispensing, administering, monitoring, or providing advice on medicines
A purely administrative discrepancy that does not involve clinical delivery or monitoring steps
An unavoidable chemical interaction occurring exclusively during multi-drug clinical trials

question: The lecture explicitly highlights that medication errors are structurally distinct from which of the following?
Which list comprises the three specific "Human Factors" explicitly listed in the curriculum slides to consider when processes are affected?
options: Inexperience, poor motivation, lack of attention
Technical baseline, communication layout, ward history

question: How many medication errors are estimated to be made every single year in England?
The avoidable consequences of medication errors cost the NHS upwards of how much money every year?
The avoidable consequences of medication errors are estimated to cost how many lives every year in England?
According to research statistics presented in the slides, over half (54%) of all medication errors occur at which stage of the process?
What percentage of total medication errors are made during the prescribing stage?
Despite covering fewer patients than other sectors, which sector exhibits the highest error rates at 42%?
Researchers estimate that nearly 3 out of 4 medication errors (72%) fall under which severity classification?
What percentage of medication errors are estimated to have the explicit potential to cause serious harm?
Which of the following medications is listed first as most often implicated in hospital admissions attributable to medication errors?
According to NHS Resolution initial data for litigation claims, which specific medication class was implicated in the highest number of claims (104)?
The Nursing and Midwifery Council's (NMC) (2018) Code explicitly mandates that nurses must execute which primary action?
options: Prioritize the completion of unit audit records over external patient interactions
Put the interests of people using or needing nursing services first
Delegate all medication preparation steps to student tiers under all circumstances
Decline medication administration if any human factor variations are present on the ward

question: Based on the framework provided by Ofosu and Jarrett (2015), which three elements are grouped together as core causes behind medication errors?
options: Weak infrastructure, poor illumination, system failures
Interruptions and distractions, poor drug calculations, inadequate education and compliance
Unclear prescriptions, wrong frequency, omitted drugs

question: The lecture presents a tragic case example where a patient died following an incorrect route error. What specifically occurred?
options: An intravenous solution was connected to an enteral feeding tube line
A patient received an oral antibiotic tablet crushed in water via an intravenous infusion
An intramuscular injection was given sub-dermally into the patient's heel zone
A nebulizer drug was completely replaced with an oral syrup mix

question: What are the strict supervision parameters defined by the NMC for student nurses regarding medication administration or supply?
options: Independent execution once the second year of study is fully completed
Indirect supervision via telephone check-ins by a qualified staff tier
Student nurses must NEVER administer or supply medicinal products without DIRECT supervision
Peer-to-peer supervision alongside another student nurse from the same cohort block

question: What is the immediate initial step that must be taken the moment a medication error is identified?
options: Complete a full local Datix event log before approaching the patient bed
Stop the medication and revert the effect (if possible!) and monitor the patient’s status
Contact the family immediately to gain explicit informed consent to proceed
Review the Yellow Card scheme logs to determine if it requires national alert escalation

question: Under the "10 Rs of Drug Administration" framework, which of the following is highlighted as a critical action under "1. Right Patient"?
options: Check the name on the prescription and wristband, using 2 or more identifiers and asking the patient to identify themselves
Verify the room and bay configuration number exclusively
Rely completely on verbal confirmation from a peer staff tier during handover
Cross-reference the patient chart against the shift entry index log numbers only

question: Under "2. Right Drug", what explicit instruction is given regarding medication naming conventions?
options: Brand names should be preferred because they prevent formatting errors
Brand names should be avoided, and the generic name of the medication must be verified
Only the localized abbreviated slang title should be verified across drug cups
The chemical formula string must be manually transcribed into the chart

question: What is the study of how the body interacts with administered substances for the entire duration of exposure?
According to the pharmacokinetics layout slide, which organ serves as the "Main" excretory organ?
What is the mandatory timeline rule regarding chart verification under "10. Right Response or Outcome (Documentation)"?
options: Ensure you have signed for the medication immediately BEFORE approaching the bay zone
Ensure you have signed for the medication AFTER it has been administered
Sign for all scheduled medications in a single block task at the end of the full shift window
Sign the chart only after the next shift nurse co-signs the verification log

question: A study carried out in 2010 by McMullan et al. demonstrated that what percentage of nursing students failed the initial drug calculation test?
The 2010 calculation error study noted that independent of status or experience, which demographic group was statistically significantly more able to perform numerical calculations?
options: Participants with less than one year of practice experience
Student nurses who prepared primarily via online formula modules
